steps.title: Calculate the starting balance
steps.content:
Original balance: \$900
Increase by 0.6% of original: 0.6% × \$900 = \$5.40
Balance at month 4: $B(4)$ = \$905.40

steps.title: Recognize the recurring growth factor
steps.content:
After month 4, every 2 months the balance multiplies by $1.001$ (a 0.1% increase):
  • Month 6: one 2-month period → $(1.001)^1$
  • Month 8: two 2-month periods → $(1.001)^2$
  • Month 10: three 2-month periods → $(1.001)^3$

steps.title: Determine the exponent formula
steps.content:
From month 4 to month $x$, there are $(x-4)$ months total. Since each compounding period is 2 months, the number of periods is $\frac{x-4}{2}$. This gives us:
$$B(x) = 905.40 \cdot (1.001)^{\frac{x-4}{2}}$$
Rewrite the exponent by distributing the division:
$$\frac{x-4}{2} = \frac{x}{2} - \frac{4}{2}$$
Therefore:
$$B(x) = 905.40 \cdot (1.001)^{\frac{x}{2} - \frac{4}{2}}$$
This exactly matches choice C — C is correct.
